item.title: Images of Note: Who turned the protests violent
item.description:
It's because people complaining about "Wokeness" don't care about "accuracy" or "realism" (see: Yasuke in the latest Assassin's creed, who was a real person), they literally don't want Women or Minorities to have any place in media or even just the historical narrative. Back in 2007, people didn't make a fuss because we didn't have this right wing "stop wokeness" bullshit, which is just a revival of the 1930s fascist war against "Degeneracy".
:O
owo
love how the last one looks like my cat.
I would imagine all of these sound like Joe Swanson.
http://twitch.tv/abbiistabbii We're gonna commit crimes >:3
You know every single right wing "Common Sense anti-woke" commentator/comedian always has that look on their face, have you noticed that?
yeah.
Space Ostrich.
King Shark in Harley Quinn is 100% better than this one.
...because capitalism needs poverty to survive because without it, people cannot be forced to do grueling, often pointless labour for little or no money.
Observe them.
Where they going? On a Journey?
The whole point of the Cass report was not to reveal truth but to strip transgender people of basic rights and dignities to enforce gender norms through state and social violence. Oh and if you think this doesn't affect you? They will use the same language and strategies to take away your reproductive rights.
https://www.tiktok.com/@cel_edits/video/7052690904922246447?lang=en
For Chats: http://discord.abbii.gay For Shitposts: http://tumblr.abbii.gay For srs and maybe shitposts: http://mastodon.abbii.gay Also Twitch? http://twitch.tv/abbiistabbii
item.dc:creator: abbiistabbii

item.title: Finally, A Step In The Right Direction
item.description:
I hate tax season, but I especially hate TurboTax. Despite TT and many others offering free options for simple filings they make it nearly impossible to actually get filed for free. I did my Mom's taxes for years - retired, fixed income, no investments, standard deduction - and I don't think I ever got through without having to purchase or lose the couple of hours of work I'd already put in. Let you start, say it should be free, then use all kinds of tactics to wrestle you into paying. The idea of a government run filing option just makes sense. What would make even more sense would us just getting a bill every year and doing away with the nonsense but this is at least a step in the right direction. Original Article - https://fortune.com/2024/05/24/turbotax-free-customers-intuit-stock/
Well of course they don't care about free customers! But as I mentioned above they sure do try to convert those who start out with a free file into a paying customer so we know why the even offer that option.
LMAO!! ::: Intuit has long lobbied against efforts by the government to offer software for people to complete their tax returns online, calling it unnecessary because private companies already offer it for free. *Yes, possible, but wrestling with the f*in website to actually get that free filing is quite a battle.
item.dc:creator: divjnky

item.title: LDS Mormon church PR employees about to get fired.
item.description:
June is almost here! LDS PR is about to get their temple recommends revoked. In my work to expose the LDS Mormon Church for the racist, misogynistic, transphobic, real estate company that it is, I have a lot of Mormon apologists engage with me. They tell me they are “Mormon and not homophobic”. But, your tithing dollars support LGBTQIA hate. The Proclamation of the FaMiLy is a homophobic, transphobic piece of crap doctrine. My estranged TBM parents have it framed (from Deseret, $500 prolly). It’s hanging in a prominent area of their house. My elderly Mormon parents have three biological grandchildren. One of them is trans. They cast the child out. My spouse and I are a safe place for the child who is still figuring out who they are. I can’t imagine the pain of having your grandparents estrange you for simply being who you are. LDS Mormons, put your money where your mouth is and use the logo in June. Use any LDS themed PRIDE logo. Withhold your tithing until the Church updates its doctrine to not be homophobic. It could be fun! Source: https://www.churchofjesuschrist.org/study/scriptures/the-family-a-proclamation-to-the-world/the-family-a-proclamation-to-the-world?lang=eng
#2
item.dc:creator: lurkyloos

item.title: You guys saw my first Comicon, I put more effort in for the second
item.description:
This was at MCM Comicon in London, at the Excel Arena
The Comicon squad I attended with…not what you’d call a particularly coordinated group of costumes
Obligatory superhero landing! Thanks for getting this far through the dump, I know it’s a lot of essentially the same photos, but I was excited to show you guys! Your feedback on the last post gave me the nerve to take it one step further. I appreciate you

item.title: The Party Tree in the Shire
item.description:
This is a lore account on a beloved tree, involving hobbits, Saruman and Galadriel
The famous hobbit Bilbo Baggins lived in Hobbiton, a village in the Shire, and the Party Tree grew on a "large field, south of Bilbo’s front door" (The Fellowship of the Ring; I; I). Above depiction of Hobbiton is painted by JRR Tolkien and it has been proposed that the Tree in question is seen at the bottom right.
In year 3001 of the Third Age Bilbo prepared a combined party for his 111th and for his nephew Frodo Baggins's 33rd birthdays. On the field tents were reaised and there "was a specially large pavilion, so big that the tree that grew in the field was right inside it, and stood proudly near one end, at the head of the chief table. Lanterns were hung on all its branches" (FotR; I; I, art by Denis Gordeev).
We do not know what species or kind of tree it was. Some have proposed that it was a chestnut, since many chestnuts grew in Hobbiton (The Return of the King; VI; VIII); if this classification is accurate it was likely young or the pavilion was huge, for European chestnut reaches a height of 20–30m (66–98ft).
At the birthday party Bilbo "went and stood on a chair under the illuminated tree" to hold his famous speech, that amused, confused and annoyed his guest (FotR; I; I). He used a magic ring to vanish, causing calamity, and departed from the Shire; but left the ring behind for Frodo (art by Inger Edelfeldt). In 3018 it was discovered that the artifact was the evil One Ring. Thus in the autumn Frodo left the Shire with three friends, including his gardener Samwise 'Sam' Gamgee, to find a solution to the peril.
The four hobbits met further companions, and visited the Elven forest realm of Lórien, where grew the great, golden mallorn trees. As they departed in the middle of February of 3019 the Fellowship recieved gifts by Lórien's lord and lady. The Lady Galadriel handed out the gifts (FotR; II; VIII, art by Ted Nasmith): "`For you little gardener and lover of trees,' she said to Sam, `I have only a small gift.' She put into his hand a little box of plain grey wood, unadorned save for a single silver rune upon the lid."
"`Here is set G for Galadriel,' she said; `but also it may stand for garden in your tongue. In this box there is earth from my orchard, and such blessing as Galadriel has still to bestow is upon it. It will not keep you on your road, nor defend you against any peril; but if you keep it and see your home again at last, then perhaps it may reward you. Though you should find all barren and laid waste, there will be few gardens in Middle-earth that will bloom like your garden, if you sprinkle this earth there. Then you may remember Galadriel, and catch a glimpse far off of Lórien, that you have seen only in our winter. For our spring and our summer are gone by, and they will never be seen on earth again save in memory.'" – ibid (art by MatejCadil)
One of the hobbits' foes was the treacherous wizard Saruman of Isengard. He knew of the Shire and sent human ruffians there to gain control while Frodo was away. In March of 3019 Isengard was defeated and the Ring was destroyed, but Saruman was contained in his tower until August. Being let go he went to the Shire and arrived late in September to take a pathetic but cruel and deadly revenge upon the halflings (art by Ted Nasmith).
Frodo and his friends returned home on the last of October. The four hardened heroes found their homeland occupied and were told about Saruman and his deeds (RotK; VI; VIII): "All the ruffians do what he says; and what he says is mostly hack, burn, and ruin; and now it s come to killing. There s no longer even any bad sense in it. They cut down trees and let ‘em lie, they burn houses and build no more."
The homecomers ignited a halfling uprising in early November that defeated Saruman's men. Then the hobbits marched to Hobbiton, where Saruman taken his abode in the old home of Bilbo and Frodo, and they saw more of the ruin that the ruffians had caused (ibid, art by John Wyatt): "`They’ve cut it down!' cried Sam. `They’ve cut down the Party Tree!' He pointed to where the tree had stood under which Bilbo had made his Farewell Speech. It was lying lopped and dead in the field. As if this was the last straw Sam burst into tears."
It has been proposed that this destruction may have found inspiration in an incident that occured at Sarehole, England, when Tolkien was at most eight years old. He later recounted: "There was a willow hanging over the mill-pool and I learned to climb it. It belonged to a butcher on the Stratford Road, I think. One day they cut it down. They didn’t do anything with it: the log just lay there. I never forgot that." – J.R.R. Tolkien: A Biography (1977)
Saruman at end was slain by one of his own servants, and the hobbits toiled to restore their land. They could not save the trees, yet the Party Tree had a form of legacy. Sam grieved over the trees most of all destruction, for this hurt would take long to heal, and only his great-grandchildren would see the Shire as it ought to be. Then one day he remembered Galadriel's gift (art by Elena Kukanova).
"So Sam planted saplings in all the places where specially beautiful or beloved trees had been destroyed, and he put a grain of the precious dust in the soil at the root of each. He went up and down the Shire in this labour.... The little silver nut he planted in the Party Field where the tree had once been; and he wondered what would come of it." – RotK; VI; IX (art by ooowyn)
In the spring all Sam's trees sprouted and grew as if one year was twenty (ibid, art by Christine Swanson): "In the Party Field a beautiful young sapling leaped up: it had silver bark and long leaves and burst into golden flowers in April. It was indeed a mallorn, and it was the wonder of the neighbourhood. In after years, as it grew in grace and beauty, it was known far and wide and people would come long journeys to see it: the only mallorn west of the Mountains and east of the Sea, and one of the finest in the world."
Some related accounts: Gifts from Galadriel and Celeborn https://imgur.com/gallery/dwKTXDv Bag End, home of Bilbo and Frodo https://imgur.com/gallery/M4z0xOk — — — — — For further Tolkien lore posts, here are my indexes: Character bios https://imgur.com/gallery/axV2mPF Folks, animals, items, etc https://imgur.com/gallery/AQK9pJL

item.title: Thank you Imgur community.
item.description: Got a new job after being unemployed for about 6 months. The job is shit and is just something to keep me going until I find something better. Everyone I've worked with so far has been a right-wing nutcase. The guy I was paired with on Friday had the Punisher logo as his phone background and kept talking about Joe Rogan podcasts. The only reason I haven't already walked away is that once I finish my training I'll be working alone and wont have to interact with these idiots. I was starting to think that the entire world had gone to shit, then you (in)glorious bastards rose up to show the world what we think about Nazis. Thank you.
item.dc:creator: TruthJusticeFreedomAndAHardBoiledEgg
